license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.rtdd6i6
title: MtDNA copy number
author: Wen Aw

steps_raw.instruction: To determine mtDNA copy number, total DNA was extracted using DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit (Qiagen #69582).
The relative fold change of mtDNA copy number was determined by quantitative PCR (qPCR) using a 72 well Rotorgene-3000 (Corbett Research, Cambridge, UK) with Sybr Green (Bio-Rad, CA, USA).

The mtDNA was quantified by amplifying ND4 and the nuclear gene Act88 and corroborated independently by amplifying lrRNA and the nuclear gene rp49 using the following primers: ND4 forward 5’-AACTGGAGCTTCAACATGAGC-3’, reverse 5’-AGCCAGAACGTTTACAAGCTG-3’; Act88 forward 5’-ATCGAGCACGGCATCATCAC-3’, reverse 3’-CACGCGCAG CTCGTTGTA-5’; lrRNA forward 5’-TCGTCCAACCATTCATTCC-3’, reverse 3’-ATAAAGTCTAACCTGCCCACTGA-5’; rp49 forward 5’-AGATCGTGAAGAAGCGCACC-3’, reverse 3’-CACCAGGAACTTCTTGAATC-5’.
The qPCR program included denaturing at 95° C for 5 min and amplification in 40 cycles of 95° C for 10 s followed by 60° C for 30 s.
Amplification was followed by a melting curve from 72° C to 95° C, rising by steps of 0.5° C, to verify that a single product was amplified.
The mean Ct values of the mitochondrial-encoded genes ranged from 10.3-13.2.
The mean Ct values of the housekeeping genes ranged from 16.2-19.4.
The mtDNA copy number was expressed as fold change of mean mtDNA copy number relative to Alstonville.
